headline: TASK-072 is the live v6.7 frontier after 6.6.0.

current_state.operating_goal: Advance v6.7 Verifiable Governance: make missing archmap impact dispositions, self-attested review provenance, and low-independence evidence machine-detectable so release sign-off can fail on structural self-attestation instead of trusting maker claims.
current_state.next_value_slice: Implement TASK-072 archmap-impact-audit: a safe_read verifier that fails when an implementation-grade work item lacks a resolvable archmap impact record with a non-pending council status. Verification gate: negative fixture (missing disposition) must fail plus unit tests and release-state-audit integration follow-up. Stop condition: audit-only scope; no renderer work, no retroactive migration; tasks before TASK-071 are exempt via an explicit cutoff. QIF boundary: audit pass is structural/runtime evidence, not semantic architecture correctness.

visible_state.speech_bubble: I need council review before I should execute.
visible_state.next_action: Submit the gate packet to council review before actor execution.

why_now.summary: TASK-072 is the best current frontier because it matches the live post-release goal surface and no stronger truth conflict is active.
reasons: The active release baseline is 6.6.0 and the runtime is now targeting v6.7.
The current operating goal and next value slice both reinforce TASK-072 as the next bounded operating move.

next_action.recommended_action: Start TASK-072: v6.7 frontier: archmap-impact-audit machine verification
next_action.rationale: This is the best open task aligned with the current operating goal and next release frontier.
